meaning: n.踩踏,蹂躏v.践踏,踩坏,轻视
examples: Trample not upon thy beseeching worms, O noble Majesty!
An angry elephant can trample an enemy to death or pick him up with his trunk and smash his hated enemy on the ground.
Companies that can harness big data will trample data-incompetents.
Also in the Agera R review (Oct. 20), I allowed poetry to trample physics.
Even though elephants don't eat cotton and tobacco, they trample through these cash crops anyway.
